summary: sendgrid-sdk postinstall beacons host, AD-domain, and CI repo metadata to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: Package name impersonates the official Twilio SendGrid SDK (`@sendgrid/mail`, `@sendgrid/client`) but provides no SendGrid functionality — `index.js` exports an empty object. On `npm install`, `postinstall.js` automatically collects installer-side identifiers — hostname, reverse-DNS FQDN, Active Directory domain (USERDNSDOMAIN, USERDOMAIN, LOGONSERVER), proxy presence, platform, install cwd and full path, Node version, configured npm registry, and CI repository slugs harvested from GitHub Actions, CircleCI, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure Pipelines, Travis, and Jenkins environment variables — and exfiltrates them as query-string parameters via plaintext HTTP GET to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping. The README claim of 'no data is collected' directly contradicts the postinstall code. Whether framed as a 'honeypot/bug-bounty PoC' or not, any developer who installs this package by name confusion is profiled and beaconed to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
